description:
dispatched_by: mayor

Stiwi feedback: bead count (2897) includes wisp/molecule step beads, inflating the total. Either filter wisps from the count entirely (match isMolecule() pattern used in status handler) or show them as a separate 'Wisps' stat. The handleStatus handler already has isMolecule() filtering — apply same logic to briefing counts. Ref: §67.
title: Briefing bead count includes wisps — should filter or separate

description: Add 7th briefing card 'Human Alignment' showing HLA archive status. Degraded mode (implement now): show 'HLA archive unreachable — pending aegis-fjsnsc' with link to tracker bead. Full mode (when aegis-fjsnsc lands): SSH to hla.lan, query archive, show recent directive count and last directive timestamp. Data struct: hlaStatus{Online bool, Error string, RecentCount int, LastDirective time.Time, TrackerBead string}. Depends on tp-z9f (handler). Soft-depends on aegis-fjsnsc (SSH auth fix for full functionality). Design: docs/designs/tapestry-briefing-improvements.md in aegis/crew/arnold.
title: Briefing: HLA status card — online/offline with degraded mode

description:
## Problem
Stiwi directive: "Some of these fields don't make sense" + "various bugs"

## Specific issues found

### 1. Internal statuses exposed on homepage
Homepage shows bead status badges like:
- "hooked: 1039" — Gas Town internal state, meaningless to human
- "pinned: 1" — internal
- "blocked: 1" — ambiguous without context
FIX: Only show user-meaningful statuses (open, in_progress, closed, deferred). 
Hide or aggregate internal states like hooked/pinned.

### 2. Empty rigs shown
"ai_gardener" appears with 0 beads in all categories — pure noise.
FIX: Hide rigs with 0 total beads from the monthly view.

### 3. Double-slash URL bug in status page
Status page links to /bead//gt-lvlw4 (note double slash).
Template likely has: href="/bead/{{rig}}/{{id}}" but rig is empty for cross-rig beads.
FIX: Strip empty rig segment or use bead ID directly.

### 4. Agents page shows raw emails
Agents identified by "scbrown3@gmail.com" and "scbrown@users.noreply.github.com" —
confusing, especially showing same person twice.
FIX: Show Gas Town role names (aegis/crew/arnold, etc.) when available.
Merge duplicate identities for same person.

### 5. Agents page naming
"orchestrator@goldblum.lan", "worker@goldblum.lan" are internal email
addresses. Should show human-readable names.

## Acceptance
- Homepage only shows meaningful statuses
- Empty rigs hidden
- All links work (no double-slash)
- Agents show recognizable names, not raw emails
title: Tapestry UX bugs: confusing fields, broken URLs, noisy display

description:
## Problem
Stiwi directive: "All UIs should be verified by playwright tests"

## Scope
Tapestry has 5 main pages that need test coverage:
1. / (Monthly overview) — stats cards, rig sections, navigation
2. /status (Executive Status) — initiative cards, work items, auto-refresh
3. /briefing — stats grid, attention items, agent activity
4. /agents — agent cards, stats display
5. /beads — bead table, filtering

## Design Approach

### Test Framework
- Playwright with TypeScript (standard for web UI testing)
- Tests live in tapestry repo: tests/ directory
- CI integration: run on every push via Forgejo Actions

### Test Categories
1. **Smoke tests** (P0): Each page loads without error, key elements present
2. **Navigation tests**: All nav links work, HTMX navigation functions
3. **Content tests**: Data renders correctly (stats match, beads display)
4. **Responsive tests**: Mobile breakpoint renders correctly
5. **Visual regression** (P2): Screenshot comparison for theme compliance

### Per-page test specs

**/ (Monthly)**
- Page loads with title "Month Year — Tapestry"
- 4 stats cards visible with numeric values
- At least 1 rig section visible
- Month navigation arrows work

**/status**
- Page loads with "Executive Status" heading
- 4 status cards present (Active, Keepers, Blocked, Recent)
- Auto-refresh works (hx-trigger="every 60s")
- Work items have valid links (no double-slash)

**/briefing**
- Page loads with stats overview card
- "Needs Your Attention" section present
- Priority badges render correctly

**/agents**
- Agent cards display with name, stats
- Card links navigate to agent detail

**/beads**
- Table renders with bead data
- Filter/search functionality works

## Acceptance
- All 5 pages have Playwright smoke tests
- Tests pass in CI (Forgejo Actions)
- README documents how to run tests locally
title: Add Playwright test coverage for all Tapestry UI pages

description: Add 8th briefing card 'Bobbin Injection' showing injection health. Show service status (up/down from Prometheus), injection stats (queries served, cache hit rate, avg relevance score), recent injections (file counts, budget usage). Blocked on aegis-nbltyp (Bobbin feedback loop must land first to generate quality data). Placeholder card shows 'no data' until metrics API exists. Depends on tp-z9f (handler). Blocked on aegis-nbltyp. Design: docs/designs/tapestry-briefing-improvements.md in aegis/crew/arnold.
title: Briefing: Bobbin injection quality metrics card

description:
GitHub Actions CI failing on both jobs (Markdown lint + Quality checks).

Root causes:
1. Markdown lint (MD040): 4 fenced code blocks missing language spec
   - CONTRIBUTING.md:36 and :74
   - README.md:13 and :74
   Fix: add language identifiers to bare fenced code blocks (e.g. ```bash, ```text)
2. Pre-commit 'fix end of files' failing — likely missing newline at EOF
3. markdownlint-cli2: 33 total errors across multiple files

Evidence: gh run view 22632584693 --repo scbrown/tapestry
title: Fix tapestry CI: markdown lint MD040 + pre-commit EOF fixes

description:
dispatched_by: mayor

Stiwi feedback: Crew Health section in briefing/status shows raw email addresses instead of agent names. The shortActor() template function exists (server.go) but either isn't being applied or the data source has email format instead of agent names (e.g., 'aegis/crew/arnold' format). Investigate and fix. Ref: §67.
title: Crew Health shows emails not agent names — fix shortActor display

Rig identity bead for tapestry.

title: tapestry

description: Add freshness indicators to each briefing card section. Each h2 shows timeAgo() of the newest record in that section (derived from updated_at timestamps, not report generation time). Add per-section timestamp fields to briefingData struct. Template shows 'Xm ago' next to each section header. Sections with no records show 'no data'. Auto-refresh via HTMX hx-trigger='every 60s' updates all freshness indicators. Depends on tp-z9f (handler must exist first). Design: docs/designs/tapestry-briefing-improvements.md in aegis/crew/arnold.
title: Briefing: per-section freshness timestamps

description:
Do the actual implementation work.

**Working principles:**
- Follow existing codebase conventions
- Make atomic, focused commits
- Keep changes scoped to the assigned issue
- Don't gold-plate or scope-creep

**Commit frequently:**
```bash
# After each logical unit of work:
git add <files>
git commit -m "<type>: <description> (tp-z9f)"
```

Commit types: feat, fix, refactor, test, docs, chore

**Discovered work:**
If you find bugs or improvements outside your scope:
```bash
bd create --title "Found: <description>" --type bug --priority 2
# Note the ID, continue with your work
```

Do NOT fix unrelated issues in this branch.

**If stuck:**
Don't spin for more than 15 minutes. Mail Witness:
```bash
gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Stuck on implementation" -m "Issue: tp-z9f
Trying to: <what you're attempting>
Problem: <what's blocking you>
Tried: <what you've attempted>"
```

**Exit criteria:** Implementation complete, all changes committed.
title: Implement the solution

description:
Full polecat work lifecycle from assignment through completion.

This molecule guides a polecat through a complete work assignment. Each step
has clear entry/exit criteria and specific commands to run. A polecat can
crash after any step and resume from the last completed step.

## Polecat Contract (Self-Cleaning Model)

You are a self-cleaning worker. You:
1. Receive work via your hook (pinned molecule + issue)
2. Work through molecule steps using `bd mol current` / `bd close <step>`
3. Complete and self-clean via `gt done` (submit + nuke yourself)
4. You are GONE - Refinery merges from MQ

**Self-cleaning:** When you run `gt done`, you push your work, submit to MQ,
nuke your sandbox, and exit. There is no idle state. Done means gone.

**Important:** This formula defines the template. Your molecule already has step
beads created from it. Use `gt hook` to find your pinned molecule, then `bd show`
for step details — do NOT read this file directly.

**You do NOT:**
- Push directly to main (Refinery merges from MQ)
- Close your own issue (Refinery closes after merge)
- Wait for merge (you're gone after `gt done`)
- Handle rebase conflicts (Refinery spawns fresh polecats for that)

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | hook_bead | The issue ID you're assigned to work on |
| base_branch | sling vars | The base branch to rebase on (default: main) |
| setup_command | rig config | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | rig config | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | rig config | Test command. Empty = skip. Rig must configure for its language. |
| lint_command | rig config | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| build_command | rig config | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Tests fail | Fix them. Do not proceed with failures. |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness for help, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | Use gt handoff to cycle to fresh session |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |
title: mol-polecat-work

description:
Verify your changes don't break anything and are properly tested.

**1. Run quality checks (skip any that are not configured):**

If setup_command is set: `just setup`
If typecheck_command is set: `go vet ./...`
If lint_command is set: `just lint`
If build_command is set: `just build`

```bash
just setup           # Make sure all newly added dependencies are installed (if command set)
go vet ./...       # Check for type errors (if command set)
just lint            # Check for lint errors (if command set)
just build            # Make sure it builds (if command set)
```

Empty commands mean "not configured for this project" — skip silently.

**2. If quality checks fail:**
- Read the failure output carefully
- Determine if your change caused it:
  - If yes: Fix it. Return to implement step if needed.
  - If no (pre-existing): File a bead, but still must pass for your PR

```bash
# Check if failure exists on main:
git stash
git checkout origin/main
# Re-run the failing command(s)
git checkout -
git stash pop
```

**3. Run the full test suite:**
```bash
just test            # Run tests (configured per-rig)
```

**ALL TESTS MUST PASS.** Do not proceed with failures.

**4. If tests fail:**
- Read the failure output carefully
- Determine if your change caused it:
  - If yes: Fix it. Return to implement step if needed.
  - If no (pre-existing): File a bead, but still must pass for your PR

```bash
# Check if failure exists on main:
git stash
git checkout origin/main
just test
git checkout -
git stash pop
```

**5. Verify test coverage for new code:**
- New features should have tests
- Bug fixes should have regression tests
- If you added significant code without tests, add them now

**Exit criteria:** All quality checks and tests pass, new code has appropriate test coverage.
title: Run quality checks and tests

description:
Verify work is complete and ready for merge queue.

**Note:** Do NOT close the issue. The Refinery will close it after successful merge.
This enables conflict-resolution retries without reopening closed issues.

**1. Verify the issue shows your work:**
```bash
bd show tp-z9f
# Status should still be 'in_progress' (you're working on it)
```

**2. Add completion notes:**
```bash
bd update tp-z9f --notes "Implemented: <brief summary of what was done>"
```

**3. Sync beads:**
```bash
bd sync
```

**Exit criteria:** Issue updated with completion notes, beads synced.
title: Prepare work for review

description:
Submit your work and clean up. You cease to exist after this step.

**Self-Cleaning Model:**
Once you run `gt done`, you're gone. The command:
1. Pushes your branch to origin
2. Creates an MR bead in the merge queue
3. Nukes your sandbox (worktree removal)
4. Exits your session immediately

**Run gt done:**
```bash
gt done
```

You should see output like:
```
✓ Work submitted to merge queue
  MR ID: gt-xxxxx
  Source: polecat/<name>
  Target: main
  Issue: tp-z9f
✓ Sandbox nuked
✓ Session exiting
```

**What happens next (not your concern):**
- Refinery processes your MR from the queue
- Refinery rebases and merges to main
- Refinery closes the issue
- If conflicts: Refinery spawns a FRESH polecat to re-implement

You are NOT involved in any of that. You're gone. Done means gone.

**Exit criteria:** Work submitted, sandbox nuked, session exited.
title: Submit work and self-clean

description:
Review your own changes before running tests.

**1. Review the diff:**
```bash
git diff origin/main...HEAD     # All changes vs main
git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD  # All commits
```

**2. Check for common issues:**

| Category | Look For |
|----------|----------|
| Bugs | Off-by-one, null handling, edge cases |
| Security | Injection, auth bypass, exposed secrets |
| Style | Naming, formatting, code organization |
| Completeness | Missing error handling, incomplete paths |
| Cruft | Debug prints, commented code, TODOs |

**3. Fix issues found:**
Don't just note them - fix them now. Amend or add commits as needed.

**4. Verify no unintended changes:**
```bash
git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD
# Only files relevant to tp-z9f should appear
```

If you accidentally modified unrelated files, remove those changes.

**Exit criteria:** Changes are clean, reviewed, and ready for testing.
title: Self-review changes

description:
Verify work is complete and ready for merge queue.

**Note:** Do NOT close the issue. The Refinery will close it after successful merge.
This enables conflict-resolution retries without reopening closed issues.

**1. Verify the issue shows your work:**
```bash
bd show tp-z9f
# Status should still be 'in_progress' (you're working on it)
```

**2. Add completion notes:**
```bash
bd update tp-z9f --notes "Implemented: <brief summary of what was done>"
```

**3. Sync beads:**
```bash
bd sync
```

**Exit criteria:** Issue updated with completion notes, beads synced.
title: Prepare work for review

description:
Ensure ALL implementation work is committed before cleanup.

**CRITICAL: You MUST commit all changes from implementation.**
NEVER use `git checkout -- .` or `git restore .` to discard implementation work.
ALWAYS commit ALL uncommitted changes from your implementation.

**1. Check for uncommitted changes:**
```bash
git status
```

**2. If there are ANY uncommitted changes, commit them now:**
```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "<type>: <descriptive message> (tp-z9f)"
```

**3. If working tree is already clean, skip.**

**4. VERIFY:**
```bash
git status
```
Must show "nothing to commit, working tree clean".

**5. Verify you have commits:**
```bash
git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline
```
If you made changes during implementation, this MUST show at least 1 commit.

**Exit criteria:** All changes committed. Working tree clean.
title: Commit all implementation changes

description:
Initialize your session and understand your assignment.

**1. Prime your environment:**
```bash
gt prime                    # Load role context
bd prime                    # Load beads context
```

**2. Check your hook:**
```bash
gt hook               # Shows your pinned molecule and hook_bead
```

The hook_bead is your assigned issue. Read it carefully:
```bash
bd show tp-z9f           # Full issue details
```

**3. Check inbox for additional context:**
```bash
gt mail inbox
# Read any HANDOFF or assignment messages
```

**4. Understand the requirements:**
- What exactly needs to be done?
- What files are likely involved?
- Are there dependencies or blockers?
- What does "done" look like?

**5. Verify you can proceed:**
- No unresolved blockers on the issue
- You understand what to do
- Required resources are available

If blocked or unclear, mail Witness immediately:
```bash
gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Unclear requirements" -m "Issue: tp-z9f
Question: <what you need clarified>"
```

**Exit criteria:** You understand the work and can begin implementation.
title: Load context and verify assignment

NEVER use `git checkout -- .` or `git restore .` to discard implementation work.

**Exit criteria:** All changes committed. Working tree clean.
title: Commit all implementation changes

description:
Ensure you're on a clean feature branch ready for work.

**1. Check current branch state:**
```bash
git status
git branch --show-current
```

**2. If not on a feature branch, create one:**
```bash
git fetch origin
git checkout -b polecat/<name> origin/main
```

**3. Ensure clean working state:**
```bash
git status                  # Should show "working tree clean"
git stash list              # Should be empty
```

If dirty state from previous work:
```bash
# If changes are relevant to this issue:
git add -A && git commit -m "WIP: <description>"

# If changes are unrelated cruft:
git stash push -m "unrelated changes before tp-z9f"
# Or discard if truly garbage:
git checkout -- .
```

**4. Sync with main:**
```bash
git fetch origin
git rebase origin/main      # Get latest, rebase your branch
```

If rebase conflicts:
- Resolve them carefully
- Test after resolution
- If stuck, mail Witness

**5. Run project setup (if configured):**

If setup_command is set, run it to install dependencies:
```bash
just setup
```

This ensures dependencies are installed before pre-flight checks.
Empty setup_command means "not configured" — skip this step.

**Exit criteria:** You're on a clean feature branch, rebased on latest main, dependencies installed.
title: Set up working branch

description:
Initialize your session and understand your assignment.

**1. Prime your environment:**

gt prime                    # Load role context

Question: <what you need clarified>"

title: Load context and verify assignment

description:
Ensure workspace is pristine before handoff.

**IMPORTANT: Do NOT run `git push`. That is `gt done`'s job (next step).**
**IMPORTANT: Do NOT discard implementation changes. They must already be committed.**

**1. Check for untracked files:**
```bash
git status --porcelain
```
Should be empty. If not:
- Add to .gitignore if appropriate
- Remove if temporary: `rm <file>`
- Commit if needed: `git add <file> && git commit -m "chore: add <file>"`

**2. Check stash:**
```bash
git stash list
```
Should be empty. If not:
- Pop and commit: `git stash pop && git add -A && git commit -m "chore: unstash work (tp-z9f)"`
- Or drop if truly garbage: `git stash drop`

**3. Verify clean state and commits:**
```bash
git status                  # Must show "working tree clean"
git stash list              # Must be empty
git log origin/main..HEAD   # Your commits (should show your work)
```

If `git log origin/main..HEAD` shows nothing but you DID make changes,
something went wrong. Do NOT proceed — mail Witness for help.

**Exit criteria:** Workspace clean, no cruft, all work committed. Do NOT push — `gt done` handles that.
title: Clean up workspace

description:
Ensure workspace is pristine before handoff.

**IMPORTANT: Do NOT run `git push`. That is `gt done`'s job (next step).**
**IMPORTANT: Do NOT discard implementation changes. They must already be committed.**

**1. Check for untracked files:**
```bash
git status --porcelain
```
Should be empty. If not:
- Add to .gitignore if appropriate
- Remove if temporary: `rm <file>`
- Commit if needed: `git add <file> && git commit -m "chore: add <file>"`

**2. Check stash:**
```bash
git stash list
```
Should be empty. If not:
- Pop and commit: `git stash pop && git add -A && git commit -m "chore: unstash work (tp-z9f)"`
- Or drop if truly garbage: `git stash drop`

**3. Verify clean state and commits:**
```bash
git status                  # Must show "working tree clean"
git stash list              # Must be empty
git log origin/main..HEAD   # Your commits (should show your work)
```

If `git log origin/main..HEAD` shows nothing but you DID make changes,
something went wrong. Do NOT proceed — mail Witness for help.

**Exit criteria:** Workspace clean, no cruft, all work committed. Do NOT push — `gt done` handles that.
title: Clean up workspace

description:
Check if the codebase is healthy BEFORE starting your work.

**The Scotty Principle:** Don't walk past a broken warp core. But also don't
let someone else's mess consume your entire mission.

**1. Run pre-flights on main:**

Your branch was just created from or rebased on `origin/main` with no
implementation changes yet — you're already at the base branch state.

Run each configured check, then tests:

If typecheck_command is set: `go vet ./...`
If lint_command is set: `just lint`
If test_command is set: `just test`

```bash
go vet ./...       # Check for type errors (if command set)
just lint            # Check for lint errors (if command set)
just test            # Run tests (if command set)
```

Empty commands mean "not configured" — skip silently.

**2. If pre-flights pass:**

Continue to implement step.

**3. If pre-flights fail on main:**

File a bead and proceed. Do NOT fix pre-existing failures yourself — that is
not your assignment. Your job is to fix the issue on your hook, not main.

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Any pre-existing failure | File bead, proceed with your work |

FORBIDDEN: Pushing to main. FORBIDDEN: Fixing pre-existing failures.
You work on YOUR feature branch only. `gt done` handles push/MR.

**File and proceed path:**
```bash
bd create --title "Pre-existing failure: <description>" --type bug --priority 1

gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "NOTICE: main has failing pre-flights" -m "Found pre-existing failures on main.
Filed: <bead-id>
Proceeding with my assigned work (tp-z9f)."
```

**Context consideration:**
If investigating pre-existing failures consumed significant context:
```bash
gt handoff -s "Investigated pre-existing failures, ready for assigned work" -m "Issue: tp-z9f
Found: <what failed>
Ready to start: implement step"
```
Fresh session continues from implement.

**Exit criteria:** Pre-flights pass on main (or issue filed), ready to implement.
title: Verify pre-flights pass on base branch

description:
Do the actual implementation work.

**Working principles:**
- Follow existing codebase conventions
- Make atomic, focused commits
- Keep changes scoped to the assigned issue
- Don't gold-plate or scope-creep

**Commit frequently:**
```bash
# After each logical unit of work:
git add <files>
git commit -m "<type>: <description> (tp-z9f)"
```

Commit types: feat, fix, refactor, test, docs, chore

**Discovered work:**
If you find bugs or improvements outside your scope:
```bash
bd create --title "Found: <description>" --type bug --priority 2
# Note the ID, continue with your work
```

Do NOT fix unrelated issues in this branch.

**If stuck:**
Don't spin for more than 15 minutes. Mail Witness:
```bash
gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "HELP: Stuck on implementation" -m "Issue: tp-z9f
Trying to: <what you're attempting>
Problem: <what's blocking you>
Tried: <what you've attempted>"
```

**Exit criteria:** Implementation complete, all changes committed.
title: Implement the solution

description:
Verify your changes don't break anything and are properly tested.

**1. Run quality checks (skip any that are not configured):**

If setup_command is set: `just setup`
If typecheck_command is set: `go vet ./...`
If lint_command is set: `just lint`
If build_command is set: `just build`

```bash
just setup           # Make sure all newly added dependencies are installed (if command set)
go vet ./...       # Check for type errors (if command set)
just lint            # Check for lint errors (if command set)
just build            # Make sure it builds (if command set)
```

Empty commands mean "not configured for this project" — skip silently.

**2. If quality checks fail:**
- Read the failure output carefully
- Determine if your change caused it:
  - If yes: Fix it. Return to implement step if needed.
  - If no (pre-existing): File a bead, but still must pass for your PR

```bash
# Check if failure exists on main:
git stash
git checkout origin/main
# Re-run the failing command(s)
git checkout -
git stash pop
```

**3. Run the full test suite:**
```bash
just test            # Run tests (configured per-rig)
```

**ALL TESTS MUST PASS.** Do not proceed with failures.

**4. If tests fail:**
- Read the failure output carefully
- Determine if your change caused it:
  - If yes: Fix it. Return to implement step if needed.
  - If no (pre-existing): File a bead, but still must pass for your PR

```bash
# Check if failure exists on main:
git stash
git checkout origin/main
just test
git checkout -
git stash pop
```

**5. Verify test coverage for new code:**
- New features should have tests
- Bug fixes should have regression tests
- If you added significant code without tests, add them now

**Exit criteria:** All quality checks and tests pass, new code has appropriate test coverage.
title: Run quality checks and tests

description:
Check if the codebase is healthy BEFORE starting your work.

**The Scotty Principle:** Don't walk past a broken warp core. But also don't
let someone else's mess consume your entire mission.

**1. Run pre-flights on main:**

Your branch was just created from or rebased on `origin/main` with no
implementation changes yet — you're already at the base branch state.

Run each configured check, then tests:

If typecheck_command is set: `go vet ./...`
If lint_command is set: `just lint`
If test_command is set: `just test`

```bash
go vet ./...       # Check for type errors (if command set)
just lint            # Check for lint errors (if command set)
just test            # Run tests (if command set)
```

Empty commands mean "not configured" — skip silently.

**2. If pre-flights pass:**

Continue to implement step.

**3. If pre-flights fail on main:**

File a bead and proceed. Do NOT fix pre-existing failures yourself — that is
not your assignment. Your job is to fix the issue on your hook, not main.

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Any pre-existing failure | File bead, proceed with your work |

FORBIDDEN: Pushing to main. FORBIDDEN: Fixing pre-existing failures.
You work on YOUR feature branch only. `gt done` handles push/MR.

**File and proceed path:**
```bash
bd create --title "Pre-existing failure: <description>" --type bug --priority 1

gt mail send <rig>/witness -s "NOTICE: main has failing pre-flights" -m "Found pre-existing failures on main.
Filed: <bead-id>
Proceeding with my assigned work (tp-z9f)."
```

**Context consideration:**
If investigating pre-existing failures consumed significant context:
```bash
gt handoff -s "Investigated pre-existing failures, ready for assigned work" -m "Issue: tp-z9f
Found: <what failed>
Ready to start: implement step"
```
Fresh session continues from implement.

**Exit criteria:** Pre-flights pass on main (or issue filed), ready to implement.
title: Verify pre-flights pass on base branch

description:
Review your own changes before running tests.

**1. Review the diff:**
```bash
git diff origin/main...HEAD     # All changes vs main
git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD  # All commits
```

**2. Check for common issues:**

| Category | Look For |
|----------|----------|
| Bugs | Off-by-one, null handling, edge cases |
| Security | Injection, auth bypass, exposed secrets |
| Style | Naming, formatting, code organization |
| Completeness | Missing error handling, incomplete paths |
| Cruft | Debug prints, commented code, TODOs |

**3. Fix issues found:**
Don't just note them - fix them now. Amend or add commits as needed.

**4. Verify no unintended changes:**
```bash
git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD
# Only files relevant to tp-z9f should appear
```

If you accidentally modified unrelated files, remove those changes.

**Exit criteria:** Changes are clean, reviewed, and ready for testing.
title: Self-review changes

description:
Ensure you're on a clean feature branch ready for work.

**1. Check current branch state:**
```bash
git status
git branch --show-current
```

**2. If not on a feature branch, create one:**
```bash
git fetch origin
git checkout -b polecat/<name> origin/main
```

**3. Ensure clean working state:**
```bash
git status                  # Should show "working tree clean"
git stash list              # Should be empty
```

If dirty state from previous work:
```bash
# If changes are relevant to this issue:
git add -A && git commit -m "WIP: <description>"

# If changes are unrelated cruft:
git stash push -m "unrelated changes before tp-z9f"
# Or discard if truly garbage:
git checkout -- .
```

**4. Sync with main:**
```bash
git fetch origin
git rebase origin/main      # Get latest, rebase your branch
```

If rebase conflicts:
- Resolve them carefully
- Test after resolution
- If stuck, mail Witness

**5. Run project setup (if configured):**

If setup_command is set, run it to install dependencies:
```bash
just setup
```

This ensures dependencies are installed before pre-flight checks.
Empty setup_command means "not configured" — skip this step.

**Exit criteria:** You're on a clean feature branch, rebased on latest main, dependencies installed.
title: Set up working branch

description:
## Problem
Stiwi directive: Tapestry should use Dracula theme. Currently uses a light Bootstrap-style theme.

## Current CSS (:root in style.css)
--bg: #f8f9fa (light gray)
--surface: #ffffff (white)
--text: #212529 (dark)
--link: #0d6efd (blue)
--border: #dee2e6 (light gray)

## Expected (Dracula palette)
--bg: #282a36 (dark purple)
--surface: #44475a (current line)
--text: #f8f8f2 (foreground)
--link: #8be9fd (cyan) or #bd93f9 (purple)
--border: #6272a4 (comment)
--success: #50fa7b (green)
--warning: #f1fa8c (yellow)
--danger: #ff5555 (red)
--info: #8be9fd (cyan)

## Fix
Replace :root CSS variables in style.css with Dracula palette. Also update:
- .status-open, .status-closed, .status-progress badge colors
- .priority-badge.p0/p1/p2/p3 to use Dracula accent colors
- Card borders, hover states, link colors
- Ensure sufficient contrast for text readability on dark backgrounds

## Acceptance
- All pages (/, /status, /briefing, /agents, /beads) use Dracula dark theme
- Passes WCAG AA contrast requirements
- Visual review by Stiwi
title: Tapestry not using Dracula theme — light Bootstrap theme instead

close_reason: Already implemented by commit a1995d2 (aegis-e2qc). /briefing handler exists with all required sections.
description:
attached_molecule: tp-wisp-8iz
attached_at: 2026-02-23T23:14:43Z
dispatched_by: mayor

The briefing template (internal/web/templates/briefing.html) exists but is orphaned — no handler, no route, no nav link. Implement handleBriefing() following the handleStatus() pattern in handlers.go. Add route case in ServeHTTP switch. Add nav link in layout.html. Data struct needs: GeneratedAt, OpenCount, InProgressCount, ClosedCount, TotalBeads, ClosedToday, CreatedToday, NeedsAttention (human-owned issues), InFlight (in_progress limit 15), RecentlyClosed (closed last 24h limit 10), RecentCommits (git.ParseWorkspace limit 20), AgentStats (per-agent counts). Multi-DB aggregation loop like handleStatus(). HTMX partial swap support. AC: /briefing renders with real data, all 6 template sections populated, nav link visible. Design: docs/designs/tapestry-briefing-improvements.md in aegis/crew/arnold.
title: Wire up /briefing handler — implement handleBriefing() and route
